project.name: MemberBase
project.description: Open-source PHP NGO member management system with accounting, CSV import, REST API and role-based access control

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Builds the application image from php:8.2-apache, installing PDO MySQL extensions, pdftk-java for PDF generation, and enabling Apache modules (rewrite, headers, compression) with a custom vhost config.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage build; application code is not copied into the image but bind-mounted at runtime via docker-compose, keeping the image generic.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Local development stack composing three services: the PHP/Apache app on port 8080 with bind-mounted html/conf/logs directories, a MariaDB 11 database with healthcheck and init scripts, and Adminer on port 8082.
nodes.languageNotes: The php service waits on MariaDB via a service_healthy dependency condition, avoiding race conditions at startup.

nodes.name: php
nodes.summary: PHP/Apache application container built from the local Dockerfile, exposing port 8080 and bind-mounting the html, conf, and logs directories with DB credentials injected via environment variables.

nodes.name: mariadb
nodes.summary: MariaDB 11 database container with a persistent named volume, seed scripts from docker/initdb, and a mariadb-admin ping healthcheck gating the app service startup.

nodes.name: adminer
nodes.summary: Adminer database administration UI exposed on port 8082 for inspecting the MariaDB instance during development.

nodes.name: docker-compose.test.yml
nodes.summary: Compose override that points the php service at the members_test database, isolating the Playwright E2E test suite from development data.

nodes.name: backup.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that proves the backup/restore cycle is faithful: seeds a known MariaDB database, runs backup.sh, drops every table, restores with restore.sh, and verifies row counts and sums match the baseline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a MariaDB 11 service container with health checks; verification compares COUNT/SUM baselines captured before the simulated data loss.

nodes.name: e2e.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow running the Playwright end-to-end suite: boots the app via Docker Compose (base + test overrides), waits for the login page to respond, resets the test database, and runs Playwright with report upload on failure.

nodes.name: unit.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow running the PHPUnit unit test suite on PHP 8.2 (matching the production runtime) after installing Composer dev dependencies, on every push to main and pull request.

nodes.name: upgrade.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow proving the migration runner converges a legacy pre-migrations database to the current schema: loads legacy.sql, runs migrate.php, asserts schema/data/tracking convergence, and checks idempotence and drift-free --status.
nodes.languageNotes: Verifies migration idempotence by asserting the second run reports no pending migrations, and drift detection via migrate.php --status exit code.

nodes.name: 0001_email_alt.sql
nodes.summary: Database migration adding an email_alt column (alternate/historical email address) to the users table, introduced in v3.5.4.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses MariaDB's ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for idempotent re-application.

nodes.name: 0002_compta_sum_decimal.sql
nodes.summary: Database migration converting the compta.sum financial amount column from VARCHAR to DECIMAL(10,2), first normalizing comma decimal separators and zeroing non-numeric values so the ALTER cannot fail.
nodes.languageNotes: Defensive migration pattern: data is sanitized with UPDATE statements before the column type change, since MySQL DDL is auto-committed and cannot be rolled back.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project documentation for MemberBase covering features (member management, segments, accounting, donation attestations, REST API), tech stack, directory structure, installation (web installer or Docker), deployment, and security model.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Keep-a-Changelog style release history from v2.0.0 through v3.7.0, documenting features, fixes, security hardening, and per-version migration notes.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the Keep a Changelog convention with per-release sections (Nouveautés, Corrections, Sécurité) and dedicated migration instructions between versions.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Developer conventions for AI-assisted work: commit identity rules, locale-only UI strings, dirty-form guard usage, CSRF token propagation, htmx redirect patterns, CSV import wizard architecture, test suites, and DB migration workflow.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Contributor guide covering local setup, stack overview, code style, CSRF and dirty-form guard rules, database migration workflow, commit conventions, and how to run Playwright E2E and PHPUnit test suites.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Design system reference defining the color palette, semantic member-status colors, typography, and component patterns (navbar, DataTable, forms, badges, cards) plus do's and don'ts and Bootstrap 4-to-5 upgrade notes.

nodes.name: MIGRATION_PROD.md
nodes.summary: Production database migration playbook: migration workflow via migrate.php, backup and recovery procedures, migration history, HTTP security headers, Apache configuration for API routes, and role assignment SQL.

nodes.name: PRODUCT.md
nodes.summary: Product brief describing MemberBase's purpose, target users, brand personality, anti-references, design principles, and accessibility commitments.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Developer task runner with 15 targets for the Docker workflow (up, down, logs, shell, db), database operations (import, backup, restore, migrate, migrate-status), and test execution (Playwright E2E, PHPUnit unit tests).

nodes.name: composer.json
nodes.summary: PHP Composer manifest declaring PHPUnit as a dev dependency and a test script for running the unit test suite; the application itself has no runtime Composer dependencies.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js manifest declaring @playwright/test as a dev dependency with scripts to run the browser-based E2E test suite.

nodes.name: phpunit.xml
nodes.summary: PHPUnit configuration defining the unit test suite location and bootstrap file for the pure-logic PHP tests that run without a database.

nodes.name: schema.sql
nodes.summary: Complete idempotent MariaDB schema for a fresh install (executed by the web installer), defining the 10 core tables for members, groups, smart filters, accounting, app settings, authentication, and audit logging.
nodes.languageNotes: All CREATE TABLE statements use IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency; foreign key checks are disabled during creation and legacy epoch-integer date columns coexist with native timestamp columns.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Core members table holding contact details (name, address, phones, emails), demographic fields, free-text comment, epoch-integer lifecycle dates, and an active/inactive status flag that all listings must filter on.

nodes.name: team
nodes.summary: Member groups (segments) with a name and a hidden flag used to exclude groups from standard listings.

nodes.name: user_properties
nodes.summary: Key-value property store per member; group membership is modeled here as rows with parameter='team', indexed by user and parameter.

nodes.name: metagroup
nodes.summary: Smart filters (combined segments) linking a metagroup id to constituent team ids, with a filter flag and sort order for display.

nodes.name: compta_type
nodes.summary: Accounting entry types with label, badge color, sort order, and semantic flags (membership fee, excluded from donation attestations, institutional).

nodes.name: compta
nodes.summary: Accounting entries per member: date, label, amount, receipt number, type reference, and a flag for requesting a donation attestation; indexed by user, date, and type.

nodes.name: maxval
nodes.summary: Auto-increment helper table storing the current maximum value per named counter parameter.

nodes.name: app_settings
nodes.summary: Application-level key-value configuration store.

nodes.name: app_users
nodes.summary: Application login accounts with username, password hash, four-level role enum (admin/manager/user/readonly), forced password change and active flags, plus password-reset token fields.

nodes.name: audit_log
nodes.summary: Activity log recording timestamped actions by app users, with free-text detail and an optional reference to the affected member.

nodes.name: ONBOARDING.md
nodes.summary: Developer onboarding guide (in French) presenting the project overview, the 13 architectural layers, key concepts, an 8-step guided tour, a file-to-layer map, known complexity hotspots, and first steps for running the app locally with Docker and executing the Playwright E2E tests.

nodes.name: admin.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive administrator guide (72 sections, in French) covering prerequisites, installation via web wizard or Docker, configuration (conf/db.php, environment variables, app_settings), Apache production deployment, user account management, security (Fail2Ban, audit log), REST API activation, backup/restore, data integrity tooling, CI/CD, and schema migrations.

nodes.name: api.md
nodes.summary: Full REST API reference (in French) for the /api/* endpoints: session authentication, role permissions, response format and HTTP codes, .htaccess routing, and detailed CRUD documentation with curl examples for members, groups (segments), accounting entries (compta), compta types, and follow-up notes (suivi).

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Architecture reference (in French) describing MemberBase's design principles, GET/POST request flows through htmx routing, architectural layers, database schema and relations, domain classes (User, Team, Compta, Metagroup, UserProperty), authentication and roles, the REST API structure, the CSV import wizard, frontend stack (htmx, Alpine, dirty-form guard), PDF attestation generation, testing/CI, and code conventions.

nodes.name: user.md
nodes.summary: End-user guide (in French) walking through login and navigation, the member list with segment filters and search, adding and importing members (CSV/TSV wizard), the member record tabs (general data, segments, accounting, follow-up), global journals and donation overview, donation attestations (PDF), segment management, settings, the role permission matrix, and password/logout flows.

nodes.name: BootstrapXL.css
nodes.summary: Tiny media-query override that widens the Bootstrap .container to 1570px on extra-large screens (min-width 1600px).

nodes.name: custom.css
nodes.summary: Main application stylesheet for MemberBase: Bootstrap 5 primary-color overrides, navbar and table theming, form section dividers, member group pills, filter buttons, DataTables toolbar styling, datetimepicker compatibility, and the settings-page sidebar layout with a mobile collapse breakpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties (--ca-* tokens), a prefers-reduced-motion fallback for pill animations, and a visually-hidden pattern (.hide/.hidden) so DataTables sort helpers stay readable by screen readers.

nodes.name: docs.css
nodes.summary: Vendored Bootstrap 3 documentation stylesheet (bs-docs-* classes) covering scaffolding, navigation, footer, mastheads, callouts, code highlights, and responsive test styles; used for the app's documentation-style pages rather than authored for MemberBase itself.
nodes.languageNotes: Legacy Bootstrap 3 docs CSS organized by a table-of-contents comment header; largely unmodified vendor code.

nodes.name: navbar-fixed-top.css
nodes.summary: Small color theme for the .bs-docs-nav top navbar: blue background (#6088c1), white links and brand text, and orange (#f9b036) hover/focus highlight.

nodes.name: legacy.sql
nodes.summary: Legacy database snapshot representing the pre-migration schema state, used by the CI upgrade test to prove that migrate.php converges an old database to the current schema. Deliberately omits email_alt, keeps compta.sum as VARCHAR with dirty values, and has no schema_migrations table.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes intentionally dirty data (comma decimals, empty and non-numeric sums) to exercise the VARCHAR-to-DECIMAL cleanup performed by migration 0002.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Legacy version of the users table without the email_alt column, so migration 0001 must add it during the upgrade test.

nodes.name: compta
nodes.summary: Legacy accounting table with sum stored as VARCHAR(64) and seeded with dirty values, so migration 0002 must convert the column to DECIMAL and normalize the data.

nodes.name: reset-db.sh
nodes.summary: Test bootstrap script that drops and recreates the members_test database inside the MariaDB Docker container, applies schema.sql and seed.sql, then writes conf/db.php so the app targets the test database during Playwright runs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -euo pipefail and docker compose exec -T to pipe SQL files into the MariaDB container non-interactively.

nodes.name: seed.sql
nodes.summary: Deterministic seed data for the Playwright E2E test database: one app user per role (admin, manager, user, readonly) sharing a known bcrypt password, sample members, teams, app settings, a compta entry, and team membership rows.
nodes.languageNotes: Disables foreign_key_checks during inserts and uses UNIX_TIMESTAMP() for date columns, matching the app's epoch-based date storage.

nodes.name: suivi.php
nodes.summary: REST endpoint /api/suivi providing CRUD on member follow-up (suivi) notes, stored as UserProperty rows: list per member (newest first), single read, create, update, and delete with role guards and audit logging.

nodes.name: loadEntry
nodes.summary: Loads a follow-up entry as a UserProperty by id and terminates with a 404 apiError when it does not exist.

nodes.name: handleList
nodes.summary: GET handler listing follow-up notes for a required memberId, ordered newest first and returned as a JSON data array.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: POST handler creating a follow-up note: validates memberId, date, and non-empty note, verifies the member exists, persists via UserProperty::save, audit-logs, and returns 201.

nodes.name: handleUpdate
nodes.summary: PUT handler updating a follow-up note's date or text after loading the entry, with audit logging and the updated entry echoed back.

nodes.name: handleDelete
nodes.summary: DELETE handler removing a follow-up note after a write-role check, audit-logging the removal and returning 204.

nodes.name: attestation_bulk.php
nodes.summary: Standalone entry point that generates donation attestation PDFs for all qualifying donors of a given year, filling an FDF template per donor via pdftk and merging the results into one downloadable PDF with pdftk cat.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds raw FDF form data by hand and shells out to pdftk; runtime capped by set_time_limit(120).

nodes.name: attestation_don.php
nodes.summary: Standalone entry point that generates a single donation attestation PDF for one member and year, computing the donation total from compta entries and filling the PDF form template via pdftk.

nodes.name: .htaccess
nodes.summary: One-line Apache rule (Require all denied) blocking any direct HTTP access to the domain class files in html/classes/.

nodes.name: compta_class.php
nodes.summary: Active-record style Compta class for accounting entries (compta table): lookup, getters/setters, save/remove, plus static aggregation helpers returning per-member type flags and activity summaries.

nodes.name: Compta
nodes.summary: Domain model for one accounting entry (member, type, date, label, amount, receipt, attestation flag) with PDO-backed persistence and static reporting helpers.

nodes.name: lookupCompta
nodes.summary: Hydrates the Compta instance from the compta table row matching the given id, resetting the object when no row is found.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Persists the entry with an INSERT when the object has no id and an UPDATE otherwise, using prepared statements.

nodes.name: typesByUser
nodes.summary: Static helper returning, for a set of user ids and a year, which compta types each user has entries for — used to batch-decorate member lists without per-row queries.

nodes.name: activitySummaryByUser
nodes.summary: Static helper aggregating per-member accounting activity (counts/sums by type) for a year in a single grouped query.

nodes.name: member_filter_class.php
nodes.summary: Static MemberFilter class implementing virtual member filters (e.g. members without a paid cotisation) that resolve to concrete user-id sets shared by the web UI and the REST API.

nodes.name: MemberFilter
nodes.summary: Static-only class exposing isVirtual() to recognize virtual filter ids and resolveIds() to materialize each virtual filter into the matching set of active member ids.

nodes.name: resolveIds
nodes.summary: Core resolver mapping a virtual filter id to member ids via dedicated SQL per filter (no cotisation, donors, recent, birthdays, etc.), always restricted to active members (status = 1).

nodes.name: noCotiMembers
nodes.summary: Returns active members lacking a cotisation-type compta entry for the given year, using a NOT EXISTS subquery against cotisation type ids.

nodes.name: metagroup_class.php
nodes.summary: Metagroup domain class: a named collection of teams used as a compound filter, with lookup/save/remove persistence and helpers to list member team names and ids.

nodes.name: Metagroup
nodes.summary: Active-record model for the metagroup table with helpers to enumerate its member teams (teamIds/teamNames), check usage, and list all metagroups for filter dropdowns.

nodes.name: lookupMetagroup
nodes.summary: Hydrates the Metagroup from the row matching the given id, clearing state when no row exists.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Inserts a new metagroup or updates the existing row depending on whether the object already has an id.

nodes.name: filterList
nodes.summary: Static helper listing all metagroups (id and name) ordered by name for filter dropdown rendering.

nodes.name: teamNames
nodes.summary: Returns the names of the teams belonging to this metagroup via a join on the metagroup membership table.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Deletes the metagroup and its team-membership rows from the database.

nodes.name: property_class.php
nodes.summary: UserProperty active-record class over the user_properties table, the generic key/value store used for group memberships and follow-up (suivi) notes attached to members.

nodes.name: UserProperty
nodes.summary: Minimal active-record model (user_id, parameter, date, value) with lookup by id, getters/setters, save (insert or update), and remove.

nodes.name: lookupUserProperty
nodes.summary: Hydrates the UserProperty from the user_properties row matching the given id, resetting fields when not found.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Persists the property row with an INSERT when new and an UPDATE when the object already carries an id.

nodes.name: team_class.php
nodes.summary: Team (group) active-record class: lookup/save/remove over the team table, name resolution, dropdown listing with visibility handling, metagroup membership management, and an isUsed guard before deletion.

nodes.name: Team
nodes.summary: Domain model for a member group (id, name, hidden flag) with persistence, static nameById/listForDropdown helpers, and metagroup membership add/remove operations.

nodes.name: lookupTeam
nodes.summary: Hydrates the Team from the team table row matching the given id, clearing state when the id is unknown.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Inserts a new team or updates the existing row (name and hidden flag) depending on whether an id is set.

nodes.name: listForDropdown
nodes.summary: Static helper listing teams for select inputs, optionally including hidden teams or a specific hidden team so an existing selection stays visible.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.summary: Deletes the team row along with its metagroup membership rows.

nodes.name: user_class.php
nodes.summary: Central User (member) domain class: full contact-record getters/setters, lookup by id or email, save/remove persistence, group membership add/remove, payment and donation status checks against compta entries, and a static filtered list query.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: The core member model of the application, wrapping the users table with 20+ field accessors, cotisation/donation predicates (isCotisationPayed, hasPayed, hasDonated), property access, membership management, and listWithFilters for the main member listing.

nodes.name: hydrateFromRow
nodes.summary: Copies a users-table row (object) onto the instance fields, converting date strings to timestamps — shared by both lookup paths.

nodes.name: lookupUser
nodes.summary: Loads the member with the given id from the users table and hydrates the instance, resetting it when the id is unknown.

nodes.name: save
nodes.summary: Persists the member with an INSERT (assigning the new id) or a full-column UPDATE, writing all contact fields plus creation/modification dates via a prepared statement.

nodes.name: listWithFilters
nodes.summary: Static query builder for the main member listing: combines free-text search, group/metagroup membership filters, and the mandatory status = 1 restriction into one paginated SQL statement.

nodes.name: export.php
nodes.summary: Admin-only standalone entry point streaming a full SQL database dump as a download using the pure-PHP mbDumpDatabase helper (no mysqldump/exec), with an audit-log entry per export.
nodes.languageNotes: Clears all output buffers before streaming so the dump is not held in memory behind buffered page HTML.

nodes.name: health.php
nodes.summary: Unauthenticated health-check endpoint for external monitoring: returns {"status":"ok"} with 200 when the database is reachable and no migration is pending, otherwise a 503 degraded status, deliberately leaking no sensitive detail.

nodes.name: bug_report.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue form template for bug reports, collecting reproduction steps, expected behavior, and environment details with a 'bug' label applied automatically.

nodes.name: feature_request.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub issue form template for feature requests, prompting contributors for the problem, proposed solution, and alternatives considered.

nodes.name: pull_request_template.md
nodes.summary: Pull request template with five sections (what, why, changes, testing, checklist) that standardizes PR descriptions for contributors.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Configuration for the Impeccable frontend-audit tool, suppressing the 'overused-font' rule for Inter since that typeface is the deliberate project-wide choice.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore list for the Understand-Anything analyzer, excluding vendored assets, test artifacts, logs, and generated directories from knowledge-graph scans.

nodes.name: apache.conf
nodes.summary: Apache virtual host configuration for the Docker container: serves /var/www/html, adds font/SVG MIME types, and mirrors the REST rewrite rules that map /api/members, /api/groups, /api/compta, and /api/suivi URLs onto their PHP endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Duplicates the rewrite rules found in html/api/.htaccess so the API works even with AllowOverride None.

nodes.name: 0F5B592E883E10E5F744B70B861AE02B.txt
nodes.summary: Comodo/Sectigo domain-control validation token file served over HTTP to prove domain ownership for TLS certificate issuance.

nodes.name: .htaccess
nodes.summary: Apache rewrite rules that expose clean REST URLs (/api/members/{id}, /api/groups/{id}/members, /api/compta, /api/suivi, /api/compta-types) by mapping them to the corresponding PHP endpoint scripts.

nodes.name: _bootstrap.php
nodes.summary: Shared bootstrap included by every REST API endpoint: sets the JSON content type, initializes the DB session via the app bootstrap, rejects unauthenticated requests with 401, and defines the apiError() helper used for all error responses.

nodes.name: apiError
nodes.summary: Terminates the request with the given HTTP status and a JSON error payload; 403 responses are additionally recorded in the audit log as access-denied events (best effort, never throws).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the PHP 8.1 'never' return type to signal it always exits.

nodes.name: compta-types.php
nodes.summary: Read-only GET /api/compta-types endpoint returning all accounting entry types (label, color, sort order, cotisation/donation flags) as JSON for role-checked readers.

nodes.name: compta.php
nodes.summary: REST endpoint /api/compta providing full CRUD on accounting (compta) entries per member, with role guards (canRead/canWrite), type-id validation, year filtering on list, and audit logging of mutations.

nodes.name: entryToArray
nodes.summary: Serializes a Compta domain object to the JSON-friendly array shape returned by the API (id, memberId, typeId, date, label, amount, receipt, attestation flag).

nodes.name: applyFields
nodes.summary: Copies whitelisted fields from the decoded JSON request body onto a Compta object, normalizing amounts (comma to dot) and parsing dates with strtotime.

nodes.name: handleList
nodes.summary: GET handler listing accounting entries for a required memberId, optionally restricted to a calendar year, returned as a JSON data array via a prepared statement.

nodes.name: handleGet
nodes.summary: GET handler returning a single accounting entry by id, responding 404 when the Compta lookup finds nothing.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: POST handler creating an accounting entry: validates required fields (memberId, typeId, date, amount), verifies the member exists, persists via Compta::save, audit-logs the creation, and returns 201 with the stored entry.

nodes.name: handleUpdate
nodes.summary: PUT handler updating an existing accounting entry: loads it or 404s, applies whitelisted fields, saves, audit-logs the change, and echoes the updated entry.

nodes.name: handleDelete
nodes.summary: DELETE handler removing an accounting entry after a write-role check, logging the deletion with amount and member for the audit trail and returning 204.

nodes.name: groups.php
nodes.summary: REST endpoint /api/groups for managing teams (groups): CRUD on groups plus membership sub-resources to list, add, and remove members, guarded by canRead for reads and isManager for mutations, with audit logging.

nodes.name: loadGroup
nodes.summary: Loads a Team by id via Team::lookupTeam and terminates with a 404 apiError when the group does not exist.

nodes.name: groupToArray
nodes.summary: Maps a group DB row to the API JSON shape including id, name, hidden flag, and optional member count.

nodes.name: handleList
nodes.summary: GET handler listing all groups with per-group active-member counts computed in a single SQL query.

nodes.name: handleGet
nodes.summary: GET handler returning one group with its member count, responding 404 when the id is unknown.

nodes.name: handleListMembers
nodes.summary: GET handler for /groups/{id}/members: verifies the group exists then lists its active members (id, names, email) via the user_properties join.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: POST handler creating a group (manager role required): validates a non-empty name, persists via Team::save, audit-logs, and returns 201 with the created group.

nodes.name: handleUpdate
nodes.summary: PUT handler renaming or toggling the hidden flag of a group (manager role required), then re-reading the row with member count for the response.

nodes.name: handleDelete
nodes.summary: DELETE handler removing a group (manager role required) after refusing with 409 if the group still has members, with audit logging.

nodes.name: handleAddMember
nodes.summary: POST handler for /groups/{id}/members: validates both group and member exist, then inserts an idempotent user_properties membership row (INSERT IGNORE) and audit-logs the addition.

nodes.name: handleRemoveMember
nodes.summary: DELETE handler for /groups/{id}/members: removes the membership property row for the given member and audit-logs the removal.

nodes.name: members.php
nodes.summary: REST endpoint /api/members, the largest API surface: paginated and searchable member listing with group filters and virtual filters (recent, no-cotisation), single-member CRUD, soft deactivation vs admin hard delete, and a /members/{id}/groups sub-resource.

nodes.name: memberFieldsForDiff
nodes.summary: Snapshots a User's editable fields into a flat array used to compute before/after diffs for audit-log entries on update.

nodes.name: memberToArray
nodes.summary: Serializes a User object into the full JSON member representation (identity, contact fields, birthday, comment, creation/modification timestamps).

nodes.name: applyFields
nodes.summary: Applies whitelisted request-body fields onto a User via its setters, normalizing the sexe value to an allowed set and parsing the birthday with strtotime.

nodes.name: emitMemberList
nodes.summary: Shared output stage for all list variants: optionally batch-loads compta type flags for the page of members, attaches per-user group names, and emits the paginated JSON envelope (data, page, limit, total).

nodes.name: handleVirtualFilter
nodes.summary: Serves virtual (non-group) member filters: 'recent' members created in the last year directly via SQL, and MemberFilter-resolved sets (e.g. members without paid cotisation) fetched by id list, both paginated through emitMemberList.

nodes.name: handleList
nodes.summary: GET handler for the member list: builds a dynamic WHERE from search text, group filters (including metagroup expansion), and status=1, counts totals, then fetches the requested page ordered by name; delegates virtual filters to handleVirtualFilter.

nodes.name: handleGet
nodes.summary: GET handler returning a single member by id as JSON, or 404 when the User lookup fails.

nodes.name: handleCreate
nodes.summary: POST handler creating a member: requires at least a name or society, applies fields, sets creation timestamps, persists via User::save, audit-logs, and returns 201 with the new record.

nodes.name: handleUpdate
nodes.summary: PUT handler updating a member: loads or 404s, diffs fields before and after applyFields to write a precise change audit entry, updates the modification date, and returns the updated member.

nodes.name: handleDelete
nodes.summary: DELETE handler that by default soft-deactivates a member (status=0) and only performs a hard delete when ?dispose=delete is requested by an admin, with audit logging in both paths.

nodes.name: handleGetGroups
nodes.summary: GET handler for /members/{id}/groups: verifies the member exists then returns the groups they belong to by joining user_properties against the team table.

nodes.name: .htaccess
nodes.summary: Apache access control denying all direct HTTP access to the includes directory, so PHP includes can only be reached through the application entry points.

nodes.name: auth.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for authentication operations: logout, password change, app user creation and deletion, password resets, and audit log flushing.

nodes.name: compta.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for accounting entries: validates amounts and creates, updates, or toggles the attestation flag on Compta records, guarded by canWrite().

nodes.name: groups.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for group (team) management: create, rename, delete, reassign, membership changes, member imports (cotisants, donors), bulk hide/show, and metagroup creation, guarded by isManager().

nodes.name: import.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for the 3-step CSV import wizard: file upload/parsing (importUpload), contact creation inside a transaction (importApply), and duplicate resolution (importResolveDuplicates), with wizard state kept in session.

nodes.name: maintenance.php
nodes.summary: Admin-only action handler that applies pending database migrations from the web UI (Settings > Health), redirecting back with htmx-aware headers.

nodes.name: members.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for member records: adds and updates User entities with input sanitization via unquote() and before/after change tracking for the audit log, guarded by canWrite().

nodes.name: metagroups.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for metagroup management: create, update, delete metagroups, assign teams to categories, and reorder categories, guarded by isManager().

nodes.name: settings.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for application settings (admin-only saveSettings) and accounting type management (add, update, delete, reorder — manager-level).

nodes.name: suivi.php
nodes.summary: Action handler for member follow-up (suivi) entries: adds and updates UserProperty records with date parsing and value sanitization, guarded by canWrite().

nodes.name: auth.php
nodes.summary: Core authentication and authorization library: session-based login/logout, CSRF token generation and validation, role predicates (isAdmin, isManager, canWrite, canRead), and login/password-change enforcement.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses hash_equals for timing-safe CSRF comparison and session_regenerate_id after successful login to prevent session fixation.

nodes.name: bootstrap.php
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap included by every page: establishes the PDO database connection (conf/db.php or environment variables), defines APP_VERSION, loads pure helpers, and provides shared utilities for counters, migration status, and audit logging.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both traditional install (conf/db.php constants) and 12-factor Docker deployment (environment variables) for DB credentials.

nodes.name: import_fields.php
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for CSV-importable member fields: field labels, allowed field list, gender normalization, and per-field value coercion. Pure functions designed to be unit-testable without a database.

nodes.name: migrations.php
nodes.summary: Shared database migration engine used by both the CLI (tools/migrate.php) and the admin web UI: discovers migration files, tracks applied versions with SHA-256 checksums in schema_migrations, runs pending migrations, and streams full SQL database dumps.
nodes.languageNotes: Records a SHA-256 checksum per applied migration to detect drift when an already-applied migration file is later modified.

nodes.name: pure.php
nodes.summary: Side-effect-free helper functions (date/timestamp conversion and string unquoting) extracted for PHPUnit unit testing without database or session context.

nodes.name: donor_table.php
nodes.summary: Reusable DataTables-powered donor/member table partial; the including view supplies rows, extra column definitions, sort order, and a row-click URL callback.

nodes.name: menu.php
nodes.summary: Main navigation bar partial with active-view highlighting, a mobile icon bar replacing the hamburger menu, and the global member search field.

nodes.name: actions.php
nodes.summary: Action router: enforces the CSRF guard on every POST (rejecting and audit-logging invalid tokens), then dispatches the requested action to the matching handler file via a static action-to-handler map.

nodes.name: views.php
nodes.summary: View router with a declarative route table mapping view names to view files, auth guards (canWrite/isManager/isAdmin), request overrides, and view-variable overrides; unknown views render a warning, forcing explicit guard decisions.

nodes.name: auth_change_password.php
nodes.summary: Password change form view, also shown in forced mode when the account is flagged force_password_change; submission is processed by the auth action handler.

nodes.name: compta_delete_confirm.php
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog view showing the accounting entry's date, label, and amount before irreversible deletion.

nodes.name: compta_delete_do.php
nodes.summary: Deletes an accounting entry, writes an audit log entry with member name and amount, then re-renders the member's compta tab by including users_edit_form.php.

nodes.name: compta_edit_form.php
nodes.summary: Form view for creating or editing an accounting entry for a member, loading the User and Compta entities and pre-filling the entry's type, date, label, and amount.

nodes.name: compta_last_entry.php
nodes.summary: Dashboard-style view of the most recent accounting entries with per-type color swatches, filters, and summary information for quickly reviewing recent bookkeeping activity.

nodes.name: compta_list.php
nodes.summary: Generic accounting entries listing view for a member with year and type dropdown filters, donations-only toggle, and totals.

nodes.name: authUser
nodes.summary: Returns the currently authenticated user object from the session, or null when not logged in.

nodes.name: csrfToken
nodes.summary: Lazily generates and returns the per-session CSRF token using cryptographically secure random bytes.

nodes.name: csrfCheck
nodes.summary: Validates the CSRF token from the request (hidden csrf field or X-CSRF-Token header) against the session token using a timing-safe comparison.

nodes.name: isLoggedIn
nodes.summary: Predicate returning whether a user session is active.

nodes.name: isAdmin
nodes.summary: Predicate returning whether the current user has the admin role.

nodes.name: isManager
nodes.summary: Predicate returning whether the current user has at least the manager role.

nodes.name: canWrite
nodes.summary: Predicate returning whether the current user's role permits write operations; used as the guard on data-modifying action handlers.

nodes.name: canRead
nodes.summary: Predicate returning whether the current user's role permits read access.

nodes.name: requireLogin
nodes.summary: Redirects unauthenticated requests to the login page and exits, protecting authenticated-only pages.

nodes.name: requirePasswordChange
nodes.summary: Forces users flagged with force_password_change to the password change view before accessing anything else.

nodes.name: authLogin
nodes.summary: Authenticates a username/password against app_users using password_verify, regenerates the session id on success, stores the user in session, and records last_login.

nodes.name: authLogout
nodes.summary: Destroys the session and expires the session cookie to fully log the user out.

nodes.name: getMaxVal
nodes.summary: Reads a named counter from the maxval table, inserting an initial row with value 1 when the parameter does not yet exist.

nodes.name: updateAndGetMaxVal
nodes.summary: Increments and returns a named counter from the maxval table, used to allocate sequential identifiers.

nodes.name: pendingMigrations
nodes.summary: Lists migration files on disk that are not yet recorded in schema_migrations, tolerating a missing table on fresh installs.

nodes.name: migrationDrift
nodes.summary: Detects applied migration files whose current SHA-256 checksum no longer matches the checksum recorded at apply time, flagging edited migrations on the health page.

nodes.name: auditLog
nodes.summary: Inserts an entry into the audit_log table recording the acting user, action name, detail string, and optional subject user id.

nodes.name: importFieldLabels
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical map of importable member field names to their display labels — the single source of truth for the CSV import wizard.

nodes.name: importAllowedFields
nodes.summary: Returns the list of field names permitted in a CSV import, derived from importFieldLabels().

nodes.name: importNormalizeSexe
nodes.summary: Normalizes free-form gender values from CSV input into the canonical stored representation using case-insensitive pattern matching.

nodes.name: importFieldValue
nodes.summary: Coerces a raw CSV cell value for a given field, applying gender normalization for the sexe field and unquoting for all others.

nodes.name: mbMigrationsDir
nodes.summary: Returns the absolute path to the html/migrations directory containing SQL migration files.

nodes.name: mbMigrationChecksum
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA-256 checksum of a migration file used for drift detection.

nodes.name: mbAllMigrations
nodes.summary: Lists all migration files on disk in sorted version order.

nodes.name: mbAppliedMigrations
nodes.summary: Fetches applied migration versions and their recorded checksums from the schema_migrations table.

nodes.name: mbEnsureMigrationsTable
nodes.summary: Creates the schema_migrations tracking table if it does not exist and ensures the checksum column is present.

nodes.name: mbPendingMigrations
nodes.summary: Returns the migration files present on disk but not yet recorded as applied.

nodes.name: mbRunPendingMigrations
nodes.summary: Applies each pending migration in order — splitting statements, executing inside a transaction, recording version, checksum, and timing — and rolls back on failure; shared by the CLI migrator and the admin web UI.

nodes.name: mbDumpDatabase
nodes.summary: Streams a full pure-SQL dump of the database (CREATE TABLE plus INSERTs) without relying on mysqldump, used by the web export for hosts without SSH access.

nodes.name: formatedDateToTimeStamp
nodes.summary: Parses a formatted date string into a Unix timestamp using DateTime::createFromFormat with strict error checking.

nodes.name: timeStampToformatedDate
nodes.summary: Formats a Unix timestamp back into the application's display date format.

nodes.name: unquote
nodes.summary: Strips problematic quote characters from user input strings before persistence; used pervasively by action handlers.

nodes.name: donors_lapsed.php
nodes.summary: View listing donors who gave in a prior year but not in the selected year, used for lapsed-donor follow-up in the donations reporting section.

nodes.name: donors_loyal.php
nodes.summary: View listing donors who gave consistently across multiple years, highlighting the association's most loyal supporters.

nodes.name: donors_new.php
nodes.summary: View listing first-time donors for a given year in the donations reporting section.

nodes.name: donors_summary.php
nodes.summary: Dashboard summary view aggregating members, donations, and tax attestation figures, including chart rendering (pie charts) via inline JavaScript.
nodes.languageNotes: Large mixed PHP/HTML/JS template; SQL aggregation queries feed inline Chart.js-style visualizations.

nodes.name: import_step1.php
nodes.summary: Step 1 of the CSV import wizard: file upload form for member data import.

nodes.name: import_step2.php
nodes.summary: Step 2 of the CSV import wizard: column-to-field mapping UI with preview of parsed rows and truncation warnings.

nodes.name: import_step3.php
nodes.summary: Step 3 of the CSV import wizard: import results display and duplicate-resolution interface.

nodes.name: members_lapsed.php
nodes.summary: View listing members whose membership lapsed compared to the prior year, for renewal follow-up.

nodes.name: settings_app_users.php
nodes.summary: Admin panel for managing application user accounts (login users), including account creation, role assignment, and a client-side password generator.

nodes.name: settings_audit_log.php
nodes.summary: Admin-only view displaying the application activity audit log with filtering of recorded actions.

nodes.name: settings_categories.php
nodes.summary: Admin UI for managing group categories, including drag-to-reorder with a client-side saveOrder handler.

nodes.name: settings_compta_types.php
nodes.summary: Admin UI for managing accounting entry types and their display options, with inline edit toggling and order saving via JavaScript.

nodes.name: settings_filter_edit.php
nodes.summary: Form for creating or editing a metagroup (category or navigation filter), rendering categorized team checkboxes and handling member moves with toast/undo JavaScript.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines local PHP render helpers (renderTeamCb, renderAddRow) that interleave PHP and HTML blocks.

nodes.name: settings_filters.php
nodes.summary: Admin UI listing navigation filters (metagroups) with links to create or edit them.

nodes.name: settings_general.php
nodes.summary: Main application settings view acting as a tabbed hub covering groups, filters, and accounting types, with a shared navigation-item render helper.

nodes.name: settings_group_edit.php
nodes.summary: Form for creating or editing a group (team), including donor-count preview updates via inline JavaScript.

nodes.name: settings_groups.php
nodes.summary: Admin UI for managing groups (teams) and their members: per-team member counts, member import between groups, inline rename, and hidden-section toggling.

nodes.name: settings_health.php
nodes.summary: Read-only admin health and observability panel showing app version, runtime, DB schema/migration state, volume counters, and last activity, plus database export and migration-apply actions.

nodes.name: settings_integrity.php
nodes.summary: Admin tool for detecting and resolving data integrity issues, such as duplicate contacts and inconsistent records.

nodes.name: suivi_delete_confirm.php
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog fragment for deleting a follow-up (suivi) entry, rendered via htmx.

nodes.name: suivi_delete_do.php
nodes.summary: Deletes a follow-up (suivi) entry with audit logging, then re-renders the member's suivi tab.

nodes.name: suivi_edit_form.php
nodes.summary: Form fragment for editing an existing follow-up (suivi) entry on a member's record.

nodes.name: suivi_last_entry.php
nodes.summary: View listing members ordered by their most recent follow-up (suivi) entry date, for prioritizing outreach.

nodes.name: suivi_list.php
nodes.summary: Form for adding a new follow-up (suivi) entry to a member's record.

nodes.name: users_add_form.php
nodes.summary: Form for searching existing contacts and adding a new member, with duplicate lookup before creation.

nodes.name: _settings_nav_item
nodes.summary: Renders a single settings navigation tab item (icon, label, active state, optional drill-down link), escaping all output.

nodes.name: renderTeamCb
nodes.summary: Renders a checkbox row for one team in the metagroup member picker, showing hidden-segment and member-count badges.

nodes.name: renderAddRow
nodes.summary: Renders a list row with a move button for a team not yet in the current category, allowing it to be moved into the category being edited.

nodes.name: _renderImportCb
nodes.summary: Renders a checkbox for selecting a source group when importing members from other groups, with a member-count badge.

nodes.name: users_anonymize.php
nodes.summary: View that anonymizes a member's personal data on request (GDPR-style erasure), replacing identifying fields while keeping the record for statistics.

nodes.name: users_deactivate_legacy.php
nodes.summary: Legacy direct-link confirmation stub that routes old delete links to a safe deactivate flow instead of a hard delete.

nodes.name: users_delete_confirm.php
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog offering to archive (deactivate) or permanently delete a member record before the destructive action runs.

nodes.name: users_edit_form.php
nodes.summary: Form view for creating or editing a member record, rendering all contact and personal fields with locale-driven labels.

nodes.name: users_general_data.php
nodes.summary: General data tab of the member detail page with inline view/edit powered by Alpine.js, saving changes via PUT /api/members/{id}.
nodes.languageNotes: Hybrid server-rendered PHP template driving a client-side Alpine.js component through data-* attributes.

nodes.name: users_history.php
nodes.summary: Admin-only view showing a member's full activity and change history as an audit trail.

nodes.name: users_inactive.php
nodes.summary: Listing view of inactive (deactivated) members, allowing reactivation or permanent cleanup of archived records.

nodes.name: users_list.php
nodes.summary: Main member list view with search, team/meta-group filters, column selection, and DataTables integration; the central navigation hub of the application.

nodes.name: users_member_of.php
nodes.summary: Handles membership toggle actions and renders the panel showing which teams and groups a member belongs to.

nodes.name: users_merge.php
nodes.summary: UI and logic for merging two duplicate member records, letting the user pick surviving field values before consolidation.

nodes.name: index.php
nodes.summary: Application entry point: enforces login and password-change policy, loads locale, bootstrap, and domain classes, then renders either the full page layout or an htmx partial fragment via the routing includes.
nodes.languageNotes: Detects htmx requests via HTTP_HX_REQUEST header to skip the layout and return only a content fragment.

nodes.name: getMicroTime
nodes.summary: Returns the current Unix timestamp as a float, used to measure page render time.

nodes.name: install.php
nodes.summary: Self-contained web installer: a five-step first-run wizard checking prerequisites, writing conf/db.php, creating the full embedded schema, seeding organisation settings, and creating the admin account before redirecting to the app.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds the complete idempotent SQL schema (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) as a heredoc so the installer has no external file dependency, and baselines migrations on fresh installs.

nodes.name: installerPdo
nodes.summary: Creates a PDO connection from the DB_* constants defined during installation, used by the wizard steps to talk to the database.

nodes.name: app.js
nodes.summary: Global client-side glue: injects the CSRF token into all htmx requests and POST forms, implements the dirty-form navigation guard, and re-initializes datepickers and DataTables after every htmx swap.
nodes.languageNotes: The dirty-form guard honors data-no-dirty exclusions and a window.__dirtyOverride escape hatch, per project convention.

nodes.name: casaInit
nodes.summary: Re-initializes jQuery datepickers, data-href table rows, and destroys stale DataTables instances inside a swapped DOM subtree after each htmx load.

nodes.name: dt_defaults.js
nodes.summary: Shared DataTables defaults: common DOM layout string and export button collection (CSV/Excel/print) reused by all table views.

nodes.name: member-general-form.js
nodes.summary: Alpine.js component for the member general-data inline edit form; reads initial state from data-* attributes and saves changed fields via a JSON PUT to /api/members/{id} with the CSRF header.
nodes.languageNotes: Kept as an external file (rather than inline script) to satisfy the CSP 'self' restriction.

nodes.name: memberGeneralForm
nodes.summary: Factory returning the Alpine.js component state and methods (init, startEdit, formatDate, save) that drive the inline member edit workflow, including diffing changed fields before the API call.

nodes.name: tiptap-editor.js
nodes.summary: TipTap rich-text editor wiring for the member comment field, loaded as an ES module and re-initialized on every htmx swap, with a toolbar for bold, italic, lists, undo, and redo.

nodes.name: initTiptap
nodes.summary: Creates a TipTap editor instance on the comment element, syncs its HTML into a hidden field, and binds the toolbar buttons to editor commands.

nodes.name: updateToolbar
nodes.summary: Toggles active-state CSS classes on toolbar buttons to mirror the editor's current formatting marks.

nodes.name: .htaccess (locales)
nodes.summary: Apache access rule denying all direct HTTP access to the locale PHP files, which are only meant to be included server-side.

nodes.name: resources_fr.php
nodes.summary: French locale file defining every user-visible display string in the $GLOBAL array; the single source of UI labels since no strings are hard-coded in views.
nodes.languageNotes: Adding a language means duplicating this file and translating the values; keys are named in English.

nodes.name: log4php.properties
nodes.summary: log4php logging configuration: root logger is OFF by default, with a daily-file appender writing patterned entries (including remote address and user) to ../logs/.

nodes.name: login.php
nodes.summary: Login page handling credential validation and session creation, with hardened session cookies (HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Secure on HTTPS) and its own CSRF token for the login form.

nodes.name: members.iml
nodes.summary: JetBrains IDE module descriptor for the project; editor metadata with no runtime role.

nodes.name: .htaccess (migrations)
nodes.summary: Apache rule blocking HTTP access to migration SQL files, which ship under the webroot for deployment but must only be applied via the CLI migration runner.

nodes.name: set-password.php
nodes.summary: Public token-based password setup page for invited app users: validates the reset token and its expiry, enforces a minimum password length, and stores the bcrypt hash.

nodes.name: backup.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script dumping the MemberBase database with mysqldump (single-transaction, routines, triggers), resolving DB credentials from environment variables or conf/db.php via inline PHP.
nodes.languageNotes: Conditionally adds --column-statistics=0 to work around a MySQL 8 client dumping a MariaDB server.

nodes.name: fix_encoding.php
nodes.summary: One-shot maintenance script repairing mojibake (double-encoded UTF-8) in a chosen users-table column, with a dry-run mode that hex-dumps suspicious rows; intended to be deleted after use.

nodes.name: guest2010.php
nodes.summary: Legacy one-off data-import page that reads a flat 2010.txt file and renders parsed member rows (company, name, address, postal code) as an HTML table using the old class-based includes.
nodes.languageNotes: Pre-refactor PHP style: direct include of resources and *_class.php files, inline HTML mixed with PHP.

nodes.name: import.php
nodes.summary: Legacy one-off import script, near-duplicate of guest2010.php, that parses the 2010.txt flat file and displays extracted member fields in an HTML table for historical data migration.

nodes.name: migrate.php
nodes.summary: CLI-only database migration runner supporting --status (with checksum drift detection), --baseline, and default apply modes; delegates the shared logic to mbPendingMigrations()/mbRunPendingMigrations() from includes/lib/migrations.php and tracks state in the schema_migrations table.
nodes.languageNotes: Guards against web execution (PHP_SAPI check) and warns that MySQL DDL auto-commits, so DDL migrations cannot be rolled back.

nodes.name: restore.sh
nodes.summary: Destructive Bash script that restores the MemberBase database from a SQL dump produced by backup.sh, reading connection settings from env vars or conf/db.php via inline PHP one-liners.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -euo pipefail and extracts PHP constants (DB_HOST/DB_NAME/...) with php -r one-liners so shell and PHP share one config source.

nodes.name: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E configuration targeting http://localhost:8080 with a two-project setup: a 'setup' project running global-setup.ts to seed admin auth state, then a chromium project reusing that storageState.

nodes.name: api.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Largest E2E suite covering the JSON REST API endpoints (members, groups, compta, compta-types, suivi) via the Playwright request fixture, including an auth-guard test with a fresh unauthenticated context.

nodes.name: app-users.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Serial E2E suite for application-user management: listing, creating, and deleting app login accounts through the settings UI.

nodes.name: auth.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E tests for the login flow itself (valid/invalid credentials, session behavior), using the login() helper directly instead of the pre-seeded storageState.

nodes.name: change-password.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E tests for the change-password flow, driving the password form fields and verifying the post-submit redirect via a shared changePassword helper.

nodes.name: compta-types.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Serial E2E suite for accounting-type settings: navigating to the compta settings tab and adding/deleting compta types.

nodes.name: compta.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E suite for the accounting (compta) view: recording and listing member payment entries through the UI.

nodes.name: db-maintenance.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E tests for admin DB maintenance: the SQL export endpoint (auth guard and dump content) and the rendering of the apply-migrations UI, without actually mutating the shared test database.

nodes.name: dirty-guard.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E tests for the global dirty-form guard in js/app.js: verifies form fields mark the page dirty, htmx navigation is intercepted with a confirm dialog, and data-no-dirty exclusions do not trigger it.

nodes.name: filter-parity.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Invariant-locking E2E test ensuring the members list view and /api/members return the same member set for every virtual filter (negative team IDs resolved by the shared MemberFilter class), without hardcoding counts.

nodes.name: global-setup.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright global setup that reseeds the test database via execSync, then logs in as admin with a headless chromium session and persists the storage state to tests/.auth/ for reuse by all specs.

nodes.name: groups.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E suite for group (team) management: creating, renaming, assigning members to, and deleting groups through the UI.

nodes.name: health.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E tests for the health/observability settings tab, verifying the system status panel renders for admins.

nodes.name: login.ts
nodes.summary: Shared test helper exporting login(), which drives the /login.php form; intended only for auth.spec.ts since all other specs inherit auth via storageState.

nodes.name: inactive-members.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E suite verifying the users.status soft-delete behavior: deactivated members disappear from listings while remaining recoverable, using a getNewUserId helper to track created contacts.

nodes.name: members.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Core E2E suite for member CRUD: creating a contact, editing general data, and deleting it, extracting the new user id from the listing link.

nodes.name: merge-users.spec.ts
nodes.summary: E2E suite for the duplicate-member merge feature: creating two contacts and merging them, verifying the surviving record and removal of the duplicate.

nodes.name: metagroups.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Serial E2E suite for metagroup/filter management: creating, renaming, and deleting saved member filters through the UI.

nodes.name: mobile-roles.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Mobile-viewport E2E suite checking role-based UI: the settings gear only appears for managers, common mobile bar elements show for every role, and guarded views stay denied via direct URL; spins up per-role contexts from persisted auth states.

nodes.name: personas.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Role-persona E2E suite exercising view guards and REST API authorization for each user role, using per-role storage states through openAs()/apiAs() helpers for both browser and request contexts.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the E2E test suite in French: prerequisites, how to run tests, Playwright configuration, the list of spec files, seed data, and naming conventions.

nodes.name: globalSetup
nodes.summary: Default-exported setup routine that reseeds the test DB with execSync, creates the .auth directory, performs an admin login in headless chromium, and saves the session storage state for all subsequent test projects.

nodes.name: login
nodes.summary: Fills and submits the /login.php form with the given credentials, awaiting navigation completion before returning.

nodes.name: resume.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E tests for the donation summary (resume) view, verifying KPI cards render and the year filter navigation works.

nodes.name: roles.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Playwright suite covering role-based access control: opens browser and API contexts as different roles via saved storage states, extracts CSRF tokens, and asserts UI visibility, REST API authorization, and server-side CSRF guard behavior.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses per-role Playwright storageState files plus raw APIRequestContext calls to test both UI and API layers in one suite.

nodes.name: route-guards.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E tests asserting that restricted views and routes are blocked for unauthorized roles, using per-role authenticated browser contexts.

nodes.name: schema-drift.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Pure Node guard test (no browser, no DB) that parses schema.sql and the schema heredoc embedded in install.php, then compares table lists and per-table column definitions to prevent installer schema drift.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs under Playwright test runner but performs static file parsing only, motivated by a real v3.5.4 regression where email_alt existed in schema.sql but not install.php.

nodes.name: parseSchema
nodes.summary: Regex-parses CREATE TABLE statements from an SQL string into a map of table names to normalized column definitions for comparison.

nodes.name: security-log.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E tests verifying security logging: failed logins and denied access attempts must be recorded in the audit log.

nodes.name: settings.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E tests for the application settings view, covering tab navigation (general, groups) and saving settings.

nodes.name: suivi.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Serial Playwright E2E suite exercising the full CRUD lifecycle of member follow-up notes (suivi): view list, add, edit, and delete an entry.

nodes.name: tiptap.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Single Playwright E2E test guarding the self-hosted TipTap rich-text bundle: asserts a ProseMirror editor mounts on the member general-data form, proving the vendored module loads without a CDN.

nodes.name: bootstrap.php
nodes.summary: PHPUnit bootstrap for the pure-logic unit suite: defines APP_ENTRY and requires only the side-effect-free library files (pure.php, import_fields.php), keeping the suite DB-free.

nodes.name: ImportFieldsTest.php
nodes.summary: PHPUnit unit tests for CSV import field helpers: sexe normalization via a data provider, importFieldValue mapping and pass-through, and consistency between allowed fields and their labels.

nodes.name: ImportFieldsTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test case validating importNormalizeSexe, importFieldValue, importFieldLabels, and importAllowedFields from the import fields library.

nodes.name: PureHelpersTest.php
nodes.summary: PHPUnit unit tests for pure date and string helpers: date/timestamp round-trips, empty, out-of-range and malformed date rejection, and typographic apostrophe replacement in unquote.

nodes.name: PureHelpersTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test case covering formatedDateToTimeStamp, timeStampToformatedDate, and unquote across valid, empty, out-of-range, and malformed inputs.

nodes.name: assert.sh
nodes.summary: Bash assertion script run by the upgrade CI workflow: queries information_schema to verify a legacy database converged to the current schema after migrate.php applied all migrations.

nodes.name: views.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright smoke tests verifying that read-only reporting views (audit log, user history, donors) load without errors.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Core bootstrap, session/CSRF auth, PDO connection, routing dispatch, page entry points (index, login, install), locales, and the shared migration library

layers.name: Domain & Data
layers.description: ORM-like PHP classes for members, accounting, groups and filters, plus the MariaDB schema, its tables, and SQL migrations

layers.name: REST API
layers.description: JSON REST endpoints under /api/ with session auth and role-based access control

layers.name: Action Handlers
layers.description: POST action dispatchers for all state-changing operations (members, accounting, groups, import, settings), behind the CSRF guard

layers.name: Views & Partials
layers.description: PHP view fragments and partials rendered via htmx partial requests or full page load, including PDF attestation pages

layers.name: Frontend JS
layers.description: Client-side Alpine.js components, htmx config, dirty-form guard, DataTables setup, TipTap editor, and Bootstrap CSS customizations

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Playwright E2E suite, PHPUnit unit tests for pure business logic, SQL fixtures/seeds, upgrade assertions, and test runner configs

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Guides for users, admins, API consumers, contributors, and production operators, plus project README and changelog

layers.name: CLI Tools
layers.description: Command-line migration runner, backup/restore scripts, and legacy import/encoding utilities for production ops

layers.name: CI/CD & Deployment
layers.description: Docker images and compose stacks, Apache vhost config, GitHub Actions workflows, Makefile targets, issue/PR templates, and package manifests

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what MemberBase is: an open-source (AGPL v3) PHP application for managing association members, groups, membership fees, and donations, born at Casa Alianza Suisse and refactored into a generic product. The architecture document then sketches the big picture you are about to walk through: a single front controller, htmx-driven partial rendering, a small set of domain classes, and a MariaDB schema managed by checksummed migrations.

tour.title: The Front Controller
tour.description: Every page request flows through html/index.php, the application's single entry point and the highest fan-out node in the graph (17 outgoing dependencies). It starts the session, enforces authentication, renders the CSRF token as a meta tag, and dispatches the request to the routing layer. Understanding this file first gives you the skeleton onto which every other step attaches.
tour.languageLesson: The front controller pattern in PHP funnels all requests through one script instead of one PHP file per page. Query parameters like ?view=... and ?action=... select behavior, which centralizes cross-cutting concerns (auth, CSRF, session) in a single place.

tour.title: Bootstrap, Auth, and Locale
tour.description: These are the most depended-upon files in the codebase. bootstrap.php (fan-in 8) establishes the PDO database connection and shared configuration; auth.php (fan-in 6) implements session login, role checks (Admin/Manager/User), and the csrfCheck() guard that rejects any POST without a valid token. resources_fr.php holds every user-visible string, since the project forbids hard-coded labels: all views read labels through the $GLOBAL array.

tour.title: Routing: Views vs Actions
tour.description: index.php delegates to two dispatchers: views.php maps GET ?view=... requests to read-only PHP view fragments, while actions.php maps POST ?action=... requests to state-changing handlers, but only after the CSRF check from Step 3 has passed. This GET/POST split is the project's core architectural boundary; the BFS analysis shows all action and view files sit exactly one hop below these two routers.

tour.title: Domain Classes
tour.description: The business logic lives in ORM-like classes: user_class.php (members, fan-in 5, the most referenced domain file), compta_class.php (accounting: membership fees and donations), team_class.php (groups), and member_filter_class.php (saved list filters). The action handlers you will see in Step 7 instantiate these classes rather than writing SQL inline, keeping queries and invariants in one place per entity.

tour.title: Database Schema and Migrations
tour.description: schema.sql defines the full MariaDB schema behind the domain classes: the users table backs the User class from Step 5, and compta records fees and donations per member. Schema changes never edit schema.sql alone; each change ships as a numbered file in html/migrations/, applied by tools/migrate.php using the shared logic in lib/migrations.php, which tracks applied migrations and their SHA-256 checksums in a schema_migrations table to detect drift.
tour.languageLesson: Checksummed migrations treat applied SQL files as immutable: the runner stores a SHA-256 of each file and flags 'drift' if a past migration is later edited. Fixing a mistake always means writing a new migration, never rewriting an old one.

tour.title: A Feature End to End: Members
tour.description: Follow one feature through the layers you now know: the members list view renders the paginated, filtered table; the edit form view renders a single member; and actions/members.php handles the POSTs that create, update, or merge members via the User class from Step 5. Responses to htmx requests return only HTML fragments, which the browser swaps into the page without a full reload.
tour.languageLesson: htmx inverts the usual SPA model: instead of JSON APIs plus client-side rendering, the server returns ready-made HTML fragments and htmx swaps them into the DOM. Server-side redirects after htmx POSTs use the HX-Location response header rather than a plain Location header.

tour.title: The CSV Import Wizard
tour.description: The three-step import wizard is the most elaborate feature: importUpload parses the CSV, importApply maps columns and creates contacts inside a transaction, and importResolveDuplicates handles conflicts detected via in-memory email and name maps preloaded in one query. import_fields.php is the single source of truth for importable fields; both the wizard and the unit tests consume it, so the list is never duplicated. Wizard state lives in the session between steps and is freed as soon as rows are applied.

tour.title: The REST API
tour.description: Alongside the htmx UI, /api/ exposes JSON endpoints for programmatic access. _bootstrap.php (fan-in 6) is the API's own mini front controller: it reuses the session authentication and role checks from Step 3 but returns JSON errors instead of HTML, and skips the CSRF guard since the API is documented as session-plus-role protected. members.php is the representative endpoint, and doc/api.md is the contract consumers read.

tour.title: Frontend JavaScript
tour.description: app.js is the client-side backbone loaded by index.php: it injects the X-CSRF-Token header into every htmx request, stamps hidden csrf fields into POST forms after each swap, and implements the global dirty-form guard that warns before navigating away from unsaved changes (bypassed via window.__dirtyOverride or data-no-dirty attributes). dt_defaults.js configures DataTables defaults, and member-general-form.js adds Alpine.js behavior to the member edit form from Step 7.
tour.languageLesson: Global event delegation makes the dirty-guard maintenance-free: one listener on document for 'change'/'input' covers every form field, including fragments htmx swaps in later, instead of binding handlers to each element.

tour.title: Install and Deployment
tour.description: install.php is the web install wizard: it creates the full up-to-date schema and baselines all migrations as applied, so a fresh install and a long-migrated instance converge on the same state (a Playwright guard test enforces schema.sql/install.php consistency). For local development, the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml spin up PHP with the Apache vhost from docker/apache.conf, MariaDB, and Adminer on localhost:8080.
tour.languageLesson: docker-compose expresses the whole dev stack declaratively: the php service depends_on mariadb, named volumes persist database data across restarts, and Adminer rides along as a throwaway DB inspection UI.

tour.title: Tests and CI
tour.description: Two complementary suites protect the app: Playwright E2E specs (members.spec.ts covers the flows from Step 7) drive a real browser against a seeded test database configured in playwright.config.ts, while PHPUnit tests like PureHelpersTest.php verify pure business logic (dates, field normalization) without any database. The e2e.yml GitHub Actions workflow runs the browser suite on every push, closing the loop from code to verified deployment.
tour.languageLesson: Playwright's globalSetup hook runs once before all specs, which is where this project resets and seeds the test database, so every spec starts from a known state without repeating setup per test.
